title: AggregatePreflightSpec
description:
Input spec for the ``aggregate-preflight`` composite primitive (WS5 #3b).

Collapses the top-of-aggregate boilerplate — ``install-commands`` +
``load-context`` + (optional) ``reconcile`` — into one CLI call. Third
and final member of the ``<skill>-preflight`` family.

The reconcile branch is the structural twist that distinguishes
aggregate from submit/status: it fires only when ``load-context``'s
output reports ``next_step_hint == "monitor"`` AND the caller supplied
``reconcile_scheduler`` — mirroring ``hpc-aggregate`` Step 1b, which
reconciles a journal-only in-flight run against the cluster before
refusing with "nothing to aggregate". Unlike submit's statically-known
``check-preflight`` argv, the reconcile sub-call is assembled from
load-context's envelope after it runs.

experiment_dir.title: Experiment Dir
experiment_dir.description: Absolute path to the experiment directory.

reconcile_scheduler.title: Reconcile Scheduler
reconcile_scheduler.description: Scheduler family of the in-flight run. When supplied AND load-context reports next_step_hint == 'monitor', the reconcile sub-call runs `hpc-agent reconcile` against the cluster for the first in-flight run_id (Step 1b) before aggregation trusts the journal. reconcile needs the scheduler family to query alive job IDs. Omit (null) to skip the reconcile branch entirely.

skip.title: Skip
skip.description: Sub-calls to skip. Useful in tests where a previous call established freshness; production callers should pass an empty list. Skipping ``load-context`` also suppresses ``reconcile`` (its trigger condition is read from load-context's output).

timeout_sec.title: Timeout Sec
timeout_sec.description: Per-sub-call subprocess timeout. install-commands is a local filesystem op (<1s typical); load-context touches journal + clusters.yaml (single-digit seconds); reconcile adds a cluster SSH round-trip (a few seconds on the slow path).
